compiled_state.title: RED test writer modifies existing tests in-place instead of appending to bottom with RED tag in test-index
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

When dso:red-test-writer modifies existing test files to reflect new expected behavior (turning passing GREEN tests into failing RED tests), it edits the test assertions in-place. This means the tests cannot have a RED marker added to .test-index because the RED marker requires the new/failing tests to be at the END of the test file.

## Expected behavior

When a RED test needs to modify existing assertions, the red-test-writer should:
1. Add new test functions at the BOTTOM of the test file (not modify existing ones)
2. The new test functions should assert the NEW expected behavior (which will fail = RED)
3. The existing tests should remain unchanged (still asserting old behavior = GREEN)
4. A RED marker should be added to .test-index pointing to the first new test function

## Fix needed

Update dso:red-test-writer agent definition or prompt to enforce: 'When the bug fix would change the expected output of existing tests, write NEW test functions at the bottom of the file that assert the corrected behavior. Do not modify existing test assertions — those are GREEN tests that verify current behavior. The RED tests must be at the end so a [marker] can be added to .test-index.'

comments.body: INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: safeguard_approval | Behavioral: RED test writer modifies existing tests. Fix requires editing agents/red-test-writer.md or scripts (safeguard). Re-run in interactive session to approve.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
